title: Willes Designs and RahXephon
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Wille's Designs and RahXephon             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 12:54 pm Let's look at Misato's crew for a moment. Are the WILL-E uniforms supposed to intentionally reference this guy? There are some slight differences, of course. Ayato wears gloves and is lacking the shoulder pad things that WILL-E members have. Or is this just a kind of generic anime design that I'm ignorant of? Still, I like the idea of Rebuild referencing RahXephon , which had many references to NGE to begin with. I suppose a pretty large parallel can be drawn too between Shinji's actions in 3.0's ending and what Ayato accomplishes in his series' ending, too. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sun Feb 03, 2013 1:10 pm Don't know too much about RahXephon, but my goodness do the characters on the far left remind me of the crew from cowboy bebop. Heck, Shinji waking up after 14 years is kinda like the flashback in ep 15 of bebop.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 1:24 pm Oh yeah, figured I might as well put this out here too as far as RahXephon goes. 2.22's ending gives us Shinji's eyes glowing red as he loses himself and battles Zeurel in order to save Rei (Only to (Seemingly) fail to save her after all, and in fact cause Third Impact). RahXephon has a similar scene, where Ayato's eye's glow red as he battles a Dolom to save his love interest in a fit of fury, SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:03 pm With RahX having been unjustly called a shallow Eva rip-off, this is a chance to put the boot on the other foot. We need one of the fine gentle/m/en on the board to give us a proper run-down of super robot dress styles, whether that half-jacket is a common design, or not. hui43210 wrote: but my goodness do the characters on the far left remind me of the crew from cowboy bebop.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:05 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:10 pm That's actually what I thought when I watched Q. Sakura's uniform in particular looks like Quon's.

[]:             Quote Post by moonwolf2024 » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:16 pm Ha...never noticed it before, but they do look bebopish. Regarding the Rah and Eva similarity. No Rah is not a total rip off, but there are some things that make you think "hhmmm this reminds me a little of Eva." I know thats the first thing that popped into my mind when i saw Rah.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 3:39 pm I don't think Rah was intentionally ripping off Eva as much as it was trying to comment on it, I think. Similar elements and blatant references sometimes serve a point in stories other than an attempt at "stealing". Perhaps Rebuild is commenting on Rah's commenting of Eva. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Feb 03, 2013 4:00 pm Mr. Tines wrote: With RahX having been [s]un[/s]justly called a shallow Eva rip-off

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 4:11 pm Not quite sure what you're trying to say with that.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Feb 03, 2013 4:14 pm If you mean: "Any newer anime than Eva for 12 years" part, i think it means quality of story, characters, settings ect.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 4:16 pm In that case, I really, really don't care about comparing the actual quality of Rah vs. Eva or Rebuild vs. Rah right now. This thread is about possible intertextual links between the works, not which series is better. But if I'm misunderstanding your point Warren Peace, by all means correct me. EDIT: Ah, okay. Thanks for the clarification.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:39 pm Anno seems to be saying that Anime has not moved past Evangelion in the years since its creation. Rahxephon remains a pointed example of this (not that you can't find much more recent examples... Madoka Magica veers off into Eva-ville in its final episodes for no good reason). I wouldn't rule out Anno commenting on its blatant appropriations at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:42 am Warren Peace wrote: Anno seems to be saying that Anime has not moved past Evangelion in the years since its creation. Rahxephon remains a pointed example of this (not that you can't find much more recent examples... Madoka Magica veers off into Eva-ville in its final episodes for no good reason). I wouldn't rule out Anno commenting on its blatant appropriations at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Feb 04, 2013 5:21 am To hear Anno speaking on the topic when Izubuchi isn't actually in the room might be more interesting... I must admit I'm not at all familiar with Brave Raideen. So when Rahxephon seems to be appropriating, say, the most memorable imagery from "Splitting of the Breast", the two shows' shared history with the earlier one is leading to this "pure coincidence"? Like I said, I wouldn't know. Maybe Raideen has a scene like that too.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Feb 04, 2013 7:57 am Warren Peace wrote: Anno seems to be saying that Anime has not moved past Evangelion in the years since its creation. Rahxephon remains a pointed example of this (not that you can't find much more recent examples... Madoka Magica veers off into Eva-ville in its final episodes for no good reason).

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Feb 04, 2013 8:43 am Warren Peace wrote: Madoka Magica veers off into Eva-ville in its final episodes for no good reason).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Feb 04, 2013 9:14 am moonwolf2024 wrote: Ha...never noticed it before, but they do look bebopish. Regarding the Rah and Eva similarity. No Rah is not a total rip off, but there are some things that make you think "hhmmm this reminds me a little of Eva." I know thats the first thing that popped into my mind when i saw Rah.

[]:             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Feb 04, 2013 10:04 am If we are going about Eva ripoffs, who knows Aru Akise from Mirai Nikki? If I remember correctly he even had Kawrou's voice actor. Liked the show tho

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:07 pm CJD wrote: Which is weird, because (assuming he was being honest) Anno had high praise for Madoka.
